understanding.goal_understanding: Navigate the level by moving the currently selected piece (and possibly repositioning other selectables/blocks) to reach the goal tile(s) (+) within the limited step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Positions were given as pixel coordinates [x,y] on a grid where each move attempted to shift the selected piece by 3 pixels in the chosen direction. CLICK on a selectable’s position switched control between multiple selectable pieces. Movement could be blocked by adjacent walls/objects (as seen when repeated MOVE_RIGHT from [15,21] produced no movement), and some adjacent objects could be pushable blocks that would move along with the selected piece if pushed; walls acted as non-pushable obstacles that could prevent movement depending on their behavior.

history.summary: Turn 1: CLICK | Switch control to the other selectable at [18, 21] to see if it has a movable route toward the goal area.
history.reasoning: Switch control to the other selectable at [18, 21] to see if it has a movable route toward the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 2: CLICK | This selectable is completely surrounded by walls on all four sides, so switch control to the other selectable which may have a path to the goal.
history.reasoning: This selectable is completely surrounded by walls on all four sides, so switch control to the other selectable which may have a path to the goal.

history.summary: Turn 3: MOVE_RIGHT | Even though the heuristic says a wall blocks the right side, we need to probe whether this wall is actually passable or has special behavior.
history.reasoning: Even though the heuristic says a wall blocks the right side, we need to probe whether this wall is actually passable or has special behavior.

history.summary: Turn 4: MOVE_RIGHT | The blocked hints may be false positives due to the large wall object; test whether movement still works by trying to move right.
history.reasoning: The blocked hints may be false positives due to the large wall object; test whether movement still works by trying to move right.

history.summary: Turn 5: MOVE_RIGHT | Try moving right to see if the adjacent selectable can be pushed, potentially opening space toward the goal side.
history.reasoning: Try moving right to see if the adjacent selectable can be pushed, potentially opening space toward the goal side.

history.summary: Turn 6: CLICK | This selectable is completely surrounded by walls, so switch control to the other selectable at [33,21] to pursue the goal.
history.reasoning: This selectable is completely surrounded by walls, so switch control to the other selectable at [33,21] to pursue the goal.

history.summary: Turn 7: MOVE_RIGHT | Goal tiles are immediately to the right; despite the wall hint, the goal itself is non-collidable so moving right should place us onto it.
history.reasoning: Goal tiles are immediately to the right; despite the wall hint, the goal itself is non-collidable so moving right should place us onto it.

history.summary: Turn 8: MOVE_UP | Test whether the selected piece can move upward within/into the goal region despite the adjacent wall hint.
history.reasoning: Test whether the selected piece can move upward within/into the goal region despite the adjacent wall hint.
